section.title: Configuring a network bridge device
section.para: A network bridge is a virtual Link Layer (Layer 2) device used to connect multiple network segments into a single logical network. The bridge behaves like a virtual network switch, inspecting traffic and forwarding data frames dynamically based on hardware MAC addresses. A common use case for network bridges is virtualization environments (such as KVM or Xen), where physical network interfaces are shared with multiple guest virtual machines.
Network bridges can be configured either graphically using YaST or manually by creating configuration files within the /etc/sysconfig/network/ directory.

section.title: Configuring a network bridge with YaST
section.para: The preferred method for creating a network bridge interface is using the YaST Network Devices module.

procedure.title: Creating a bridge interface with YaST

step.para: Use Add and change the Device Type to Bridge. Proceed with Next.
Enter a configuration name in the Configuration Name field (for example, br0) and click Next.
In the Address tab, specify how the network bridge interface obtains its IP configuration (either via DHCP or by assigning a Statically Assigned IP Address).
Switch to the Bridged Devices tab. Select the checkbox next to the physical network card or VLAN interface that you want to include as a port inside the bridge matrix.
Click Next to return to the Network Settings overview, then click OK to save the configuration and restart the network service.

section.title: Manually configure network bridge
section.para: If you choose to bypass YaST, you can configure a network bridge manually by modifying the control parameters inside the network directory scripts. In this scenario, we assume a logical bridge interface named br0 is created to encapsulate a physical interface named eth0.

procedure.title: Configuring a bridge with wicked interface scripts

step.para: Create a new configuration script profile for the virtual bridge target at /etc/sysconfig/network/ifcfg-br0:
step.screen: STARTMODE='auto' BOOTPROTO='static' IPADDR='IP_ADDRESS_OF_THE_BRIDGE' BRIDGE='yes' BRIDGE_PORTS='eth0' BRIDGE_STP='off'

step.para: Modify the properties of the underlying physical network card profile (for example, /etc/sysconfig/network/ifcfg-eth0). Ensure that any existing IP address attributes are deleted, and disable protocol configuration options so that it acts purely as a physical port layer:
If using a static IP topology, map the default gateway explicitly to the bridge network interface using a dedicated routing file profile at /etc/sysconfig/network/ifroute-br0:

warning.title: Conflicting Router Configurations
warning.para: Verify that no obsolete routing statements remain active inside your physical interface script files (such as ifroute-eth0). Failing to remove default paths from physical slave adapters will create structural routing conflict flags inside the kernel.

step.para: Reload the active networking configuration space to enforce the changes:
